_note: SAMPLE / DEMO DATA — career paths for the Pathfinder decision engine. Pay and outlook are sourced from the BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook (May 2024 medians and 2024–34 projections, as labeled per figure) and O*NET OnLine; figures change over time — verify current numbers at bls.gov/ooh. Entrepreneur-track figures use the closest proxy occupation (no SOC code exists for business ownership). attrs are 0–5 on: hands, tech, people, data, lead, risk, physical, outdoor, care, autonomy. speed = time to first real paycheck on this path.

careers.label: Cybersecurity analyst
careers.blurb: Defend networks and hunt threats — clearances and military discipline are prized here.
careers.outlookSample: Much faster than average (29% growth projected 2024–34)
careers.whyVets: Security clearances, OPSEC mindset, and 24/7 ops experience translate directly.
entryPath: Earn CompTIA Security+ (weeks of study; GI Bill can reimburse cert exams).
Use a SkillBridge internship at a security operations center if still in service.
Leverage your clearance for defense-sector SOC analyst roles.
Build toward CySA+/CISSP as you gain experience.

careers.label: IT support & networking
careers.blurb: Keep organizations running — the fastest on-ramp into tech careers.
careers.paySample: $60,340 (May 2024 median, BLS — computer user support specialists)
careers.outlookSample: Projected to decline 3% 2024–34; still ~50,500 openings/yr from replacement needs
careers.whyVets: Comms and sig support experience maps directly; employers value calm troubleshooting under pressure.
entryPath: Earn CompTIA A+ and Network+ (self-study or a short VA-approved course).
Apply for help-desk / field-tech roles at veteran-friendly employers.
Stack Azure/Cisco certs on the job to level up fast.

careers.label: Project / operations manager
careers.blurb: Run schedules, budgets, and teams — what NCOs and officers already do, in civilian clothes.
careers.whyVets: Leading people, logistics, and missions IS project management — add the civilian vocabulary and certification.
entryPath: Translate your leadership into PM language (scope, schedule, budget, stakeholders).
Earn CAPM or PMP (PMI offers military discounts; GI Bill reimburses exams).
Target ops/PM roles via SkillBridge fellowships (e.g., Hiring Our Heroes).

careers.label: Federal civil service
careers.blurb: Stable GS career with veterans' preference, pension, and mission continuity.
careers.paySample: $101,190 (May 2024 median, BLS — management analysts, used as proxy; GS pay varies by grade/locality)
careers.outlookSample: Much faster than average (9% growth projected 2024–34 — proxy occupation)
careers.whyVets: Veterans' preference points, buy-back of military time toward retirement, and familiar structure.
entryPath: Build a FEDERAL-format resume (much longer and more detailed than civilian).
Set up USAJOBS with saved searches; claim veterans' preference.
Ask about your military service buy-back for federal retirement.
mosBridges: All branches/MOS — admin, logistics, security, and analyst roles especially

careers.label: Healthcare (EMT → RN paths)
careers.blurb: Turn medic/corpsman experience into licensed healthcare careers with real credit for your training.
careers.paySample: $93,600 (May 2024 median, BLS — registered nurses; EMT entry rungs pay less)
careers.outlookSample: Faster than average (5% growth projected 2024–34); ~189,100 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: 68W/HM training often converts to EMT credit and advanced placement in nursing bridge programs.
entryPath: Pull your Joint Services Transcript — medic training often equals real credit.
Test out of EMT where your state allows military-to-EMT bridges.
Use the GI Bill for an LPN/RN bridge program with housing allowance.
Ask VA facilities about veteran hiring for clinical roles.

careers.label: Teaching & training
careers.blurb: Bring discipline and mentorship to classrooms or corporate training rooms.
careers.outlookSample: Projected to decline 2% 2024–34; still ~66,200 openings/yr from replacement needs
careers.whyVets: Military instructors already teach for a living; many states run alternative certification routes for veterans.
entryPath: Check your state's alternative teacher certification for veterans.
Use the GI Bill to finish the required degree/credential.
Consider corporate training / instructional design if you prefer industry — openings there aren't tied to school-district hiring cycles.

careers.label: Business analyst (degree path)
careers.blurb: A GI Bill business/analytics degree into corporate strategy, finance, or consulting.
careers.outlookSample: Much faster than average (9% growth projected 2024–34); ~98,100 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: Vets bring ops context analysts usually lack; Yellow Ribbon schools + SVA chapters smooth the path.
entryPath: Use Smart transcript credit to shorten the degree.
Pick a VA-approved program; compare Yellow Ribbon participation.
Intern via veteran fellowship programs junior year.

careers.label: Electrician
careers.blurb: Earn-while-you-learn apprenticeship into a licensed, always-in-demand trade.
careers.outlookSample: Much faster than average (9% growth projected 2024–34); ~81,000 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: Helmets to Hardhats places veterans into union apprenticeships; the GI Bill pays a housing stipend on top of apprentice wages.
entryPath: Apply through Helmets to Hardhats or a local IBEW apprenticeship.
Register the apprenticeship with the VA to draw the GI Bill housing stipend while you earn.
License as journeyman (~4 yrs) — then master/contractor if you want your own shop.

careers.label: HVAC technician
careers.blurb: Heating and cooling never goes out of demand — fast certification, steady work, path to your own business.
careers.outlookSample: Much faster than average (8% growth projected 2024–34); ~40,100 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: Mechanical MOS experience shortens training; many vets later launch their own HVAC companies.
entryPath: Complete a short VA-approved HVAC program or apprenticeship.
Earn EPA 608 certification (required for refrigerants).
Join a shop; consider your own truck after 3–5 years (see Entrepreneur track).

careers.label: Welder / fabricator
careers.blurb: Certified welders stay booked — shipyards, pipelines, and fab shops actively recruit veterans.
careers.outlookSample: Slower than average (2% growth projected 2024–34); ~45,600 openings/yr, mostly replacement-driven
careers.whyVets: Short cert path, clear skill tests, and shift discipline vets already have.
entryPath: Enroll in a VA-approved welding program (AWS certs).
Certify in MIG/TIG/stick; add pipe welding for the top pay tiers.
Choose shop stability or high-pay travel contracts.

careers.label: Aviation maintenance (A&P)
careers.blurb: Military aircraft experience can count toward the FAA A&P license — airlines are hiring hard.
careers.paySample: $78,680 (May 2024 median, BLS — aircraft mechanics and service technicians; avionics technicians $81,390)
careers.outlookSample: Faster than average (5% growth projected 2024–34); ~13,100 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: Documented military maintenance time can authorize you to test for the FAA A&P — sometimes without further school.
entryPath: Ask the FAA about crediting your documented military maintenance experience toward A&P testing.
Fill any gaps with a VA-approved A&P program.
Target airline/MRO veteran hiring programs.

careers.label: CDL / logistics driver
careers.blurb: The fastest paycheck on this list — military driving experience can even waive the road test.
careers.outlookSample: As fast as average (4% growth projected 2024–34); ~237,600 openings/yr
careers.whyVets: FMCSA's Military Skills Test Waiver can skip the CDL road test for qualified military drivers; GI Bill covers CDL school.
entryPath: Check the FMCSA Military Skills Test Waiver if you drove heavy vehicles in service.
Otherwise use a GI Bill-approved CDL school (weeks, not months).
Choose local/regional/OTR based on the home life you want.

careers.label: Franchise owner
careers.blurb: Run your own operation with a proven playbook — franchising rewards people who execute systems.
careers.paySample: $102,950 (May 2024 median, BLS — general and operations managers; owner income varies with the business)
careers.outlookSample: As fast as average (4% growth for top executives projected 2024–34 — proxy occupation)
careers.whyVets: Many franchisors offer veteran fee discounts (VetFran); vets thrive on documented systems.
entryPath: Take SBA Boots to Business (free) to pressure-test the idea.
Browse VetFran-listed brands with veteran incentives.
Build the funding plan with a VBOC advisor + SBA veteran loan options.
Validate the local market before signing anything.
mosBridges: Any — ops and logistics backgrounds shine

careers.label: Service business owner
careers.blurb: Start lean — landscaping, hauling, inspections, consulting, trades services — and grow on your terms.
careers.paySample: $102,950 (May 2024 median, BLS — general and operations managers; owner income varies widely)
careers.outlookSample: As fast as average (4% growth for top executives projected 2024–34 — proxy occupation)
careers.whyVets: Low startup cost, immediate revenue, and total autonomy — the classic first veteran business.
entryPath: Write a one-page plan with a VBOC advisor (free).
Take Boots to Business; validate demand with 10 real customer conversations.
Set up LLC, insurance, and simple bookkeeping.
Land the first 3 customers before buying anything expensive.
mosBridges: Any — trade + people skills combos are strongest

careers.label: Federal contracting (SDVOSB)
careers.blurb: Service-disabled veteran-owned businesses can win set-aside federal contracts — a real structural edge.
careers.paySample: $102,950 (May 2024 median, BLS — general and operations managers; contract revenue varies)
careers.outlookSample: As fast as average (4% growth for top executives projected 2024–34 — proxy occupation)
careers.whyVets: SDVOSB set-asides + your niche expertise = a defensible lane most founders can't enter.
entryPath: Confirm SDVOSB eligibility and register the entity (SBA VetCert).
Register in SAM.gov; pick target NAICS codes from your expertise.
Start as a subcontractor / mentor-protégé to learn the game.
Bid small set-asides; build past performance.
mosBridges: Any — pairs best with your technical specialty
